name: Gorditas (Pyles)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the masa harina, lard, salt, flour, and baking powder in a bowl.
Mix thoroughly.
2. Divide the dough into 16 equal ball-shaped portions to make gorditas,
or 8 portions to make gordas (the larger version).
3. Press each dough portion out with a tortilla press lined with lightly oiled
plastic wrap to form a disk about 1/8-inch thick.
4. Heat enough oil in a frying pan so it measures 1/2 inch up the side.
Heat until just barely smoking over medium heat, or until the oil reaches
350°F (175°C).
5. Preheat a comal or skillet over medium-high heat for about 5 minutes,
until very hot.
6. Carefully place 3-4 gorditas at a time in the hot oil and fry until browned
on one side, about 30 seconds per side.
7. Using tongs, flip the gorditas to brown the other side as well.
Remove when puffed and crispy.
8. Drain the gorditas on paper towels until cool before serving.
